event: Christ Walks on Water and Rescues Peter
symbols: stormy sea — the trials and tribulations that threaten to overwhelm faith
Christ standing on water — divine sovereignty over the forces of chaos and nature
Peter sinking — the failure of faith when eyes turn from Christ to circumstances
outstretched hands — Christ reaching to rescue the faltering disciple
boat with disciples — the Church tossed by storms yet preserved by Christ's presence
description: A dramatic scene on a dark, stormy sea. At right, a figure in white robes with a golden halo stands calmly on the churning water. Before him, an older bearded man with a faint halo sinks into the waves, reaching upward desperately. In the background at left, a boat with a dark green sail carries several figures, partially obscured by the turbulent conditions. The sky is dark and threatening with purple-gray storm clouds. The sea is rendered in deep greens and blues.
theological_description: Christ walking on the water (Matthew 14:22-33) is a theophany — a revelation of divine identity. In the Old Testament, treading on the waves is an attribute of God alone (Job 9:8; Psalm 77:19). When Jesus walks on the sea, He manifests His divine nature. Peter's attempt to walk to Jesus represents the boldness of faith, while his sinking reveals its fragility: 'When he saw the wind, he was afraid' (Matthew 14:30). The moment depicted — Christ reaching down to the sinking Peter — captures the Gospel in miniature: human faith falters, but divine grace reaches down to save. Jesus's gentle rebuke, 'O you of little faith, why did you doubt?', is not a condemnation but an invitation to deeper trust. The boat represents the Church, always storm-tossed yet never sinking because Christ is present. This scene has consoled believers through every age of persecution and trial.

scripture_refs.reason: Jesus walking on the water and rescuing the sinking Peter — the exact scene depicted
Mark's parallel account of Christ walking on the sea
God alone treads on the waves of the sea — Jesus's act reveals His divine identity
Save me, O God, for the waters have come up to my neck — Peter's cry from the deep
When you pass through the waters I will be with you — God's promise of presence in the storm
